source.description: Six-panel marker process contact sheet generated for a locked Doodlea.day current lesson, then cropped into public step frames and a finished image.
source.notes: Chosen for July 4 because backyard grilling is a natural seasonal hook and avoids repeating recent firecracker, firework, popsicle, beach, drum, and sticker-face lessons.

process_strategy: A six-panel process builds a rounded kettle grill from bowl and lid shapes, then adds legs, wheels, grate, smoke curls, marker fills, and final outline cleanup.

final_elements.name: round grill bowl and lid
final_elements.description: A simple round kettle grill body with a domed lid shape.

final_elements.name: short legs and wheels
final_elements.description: Three short supports with two small wheels under the grill.

final_elements.name: handle and vent
final_elements.description: A lid handle and small vent marks on the domed top.

final_elements.name: grate and flame marks
final_elements.description: A simple grate line and small flame shapes inside the open front area.

final_elements.name: smoke curls
final_elements.description: A few curved smoke lines rising from the grill.

final_elements.name: red and charcoal marker fills
final_elements.description: Bold red marker on the grill shell, dark charcoal legs and grate, orange flames, and gray smoke.

final_elements.name: white shine gaps
final_elements.description: Small uncolored or gel-pen shine gaps on the existing grill shell.

frames.visible_job: Places the round grill bowl and domed lid guide.
Adds short supports and small wheels under the existing grill body.
Adds the lid handle, vent dots, and clearer keeper outline.
Adds the grate, flame shapes, and smoke curls to existing grill parts.
Adds red, charcoal, orange, gray, and shine accents only to shapes already present.

final_step.visible_job: Only thickens the existing black outlines, deepens the marker fills, sharpens shine gaps, and strengthens the sticker shadow.

rejection_checks: No new leg, wheel, handle, grate, smoke curl, flame, or color area should first appear in the final image.
The grill must keep the same rounded kettle shape, angle, and scale in every frame.
The finish must look like a handmade marker doodle, not a clean vector icon or polished poster.
